btx-precision.opportunity: BTX Precision scores a low 20 on opportunity, with only the $5.6M government contract award (sig-0005) driving upside. That is heavily outweighed by risk at 79 and competitive pressure at 71, fueled by a quality escape, a $16.7M recompete loss, a competitor win, and repeated price increases. Despite a perfect 100% capability fit (AS9100, precision machining, titanium, no gaps), the account is under active threat and needs defensive attention before it can be grown.

findings.sig-0001: A quality escape triggered a customer source-inspection hold, adding 20 to risk and 10 to competitive pressure — this reputation hit must be contained before pursuing new work.
findings.sig-0002: Ironclad Components won a deal BTX was chasing, the single largest competitive-pressure driver at 30 — expect Ironclad to keep encroaching on this account.
findings.sig-0003: A price increase citing material and labor inflation adds 15 to risk and 10 to competitive pressure — higher quotes make BTX more vulnerable to lower-cost rivals.
findings.sig-0004: A second inflation-driven price increase adds 9 to risk and 6 to competitive pressure — compounding pricing exposure that widens the gap versus competitors on cost.
findings.sig-0005: A $5.6M government contract award is the only positive, providing the full opportunity score of 20 — build on this win to offset the losses elsewhere.
findings.sig-0006: Losing a long-held $16.7M recompete adds 20 to risk and 15 to competitive pressure — the biggest revenue threat, signaling erosion of an established base of business.

titan-castings.opportunity: Titan Castings scores 0 on opportunity, driven entirely by regulatory risk (29) from three separate regulatory_change signals hitting their certified processes. With a 67% fit — BTX can serve their AS9100 and aluminum needs but has a NADCAP gap — this is a compliance-pressure play: Titan is facing new requirements on certified processes and may need a qualified partner to absorb or backstop affected work. The AS9100/aluminum overlap makes BTX a credible option, though the NADCAP gap must be addressed before pursuing regulated scope.

findings.sig-0007: New compliance requirements on Titan's certified processes (weighted +15) — the largest risk driver; position BTX's AS9100/aluminum capability as a compliant fallback for affected work.
findings.sig-0008: A second regulatory change (+9) on certified processes reinforces mounting compliance pressure Titan must resolve.
findings.sig-0009: A third regulatory change (+5) shows a recurring pattern of certification disruption — open the conversation around process qualification support, noting BTX's NADCAP gap on any regulated scope.

meridian-forgings.opportunity: Meridian Forgings scores 0 on opportunity but is a strong 80% fit for BTX Precision, capable of 5-axis CNC, build-to-print, precision machining, and titanium work. Two active pain signals drive the risk profile: extended lead times point to a floor near capacity (capacityRisk=35), and a recent quality escape triggered a customer source-inspection hold (competitivePressure=10, total risk=50). These stress points create an opening for BTX to step in as an alternate or overflow supplier where Meridian is stretched.

findings.sig-0010: Meridian's near-capacity floor and extended lead times mean their customers may need overflow capacity — position BTX for 5-axis CNC, precision machining, and titanium work Meridian can't turn quickly.
findings.sig-0011: The quality escape and source-inspection hold signal a trust gap with Meridian's customers — pitch BTX's precision and quality track record as a reliable second source.

cobalt-alloys.opportunity: Cobalt Alloys scores 0 on opportunity because the signals point to a struggling supplier, not a viable prospect: high risk (60) driven by delivery slips, quality problems, and price hikes, plus capacity risk (15) and competitive pressure (20). Fit is only 67% — BTX can cover ITAR and aluminum work but has a NADCAP gap. Rather than a sales target, this is a supplier BTX should treat with caution or view as a source of displaced business its customers may be seeking to replace.

findings.sig-0013: Cobalt Alloys' multi-week shipment slip (supplier_delay +25 risk, +15 capacity risk) signals unreliable delivery — a red flag if BTX relies on them, and an opening if their customers need a dependable alternative.
findings.sig-0014: Cobalt Alloys' price increases (pricing_pressure +15 risk, +10 competitive pressure) mean their customers face rising costs and may be shopping for better-priced capacity.
findings.sig-0015: The quality escape and resulting source-inspection hold (quality_escape +20 risk, +10 competitive pressure) show quality control problems that erode customer trust and create room for a more reliable supplier.

granite-tooling.opportunity: Granite Tooling scores 0 on opportunity, driven by high risk (53) and competitive pressure (20) with a weak fit at only 33% — BTX can serve aluminum but has clear gaps in assembly and sheet metal. Repeated pricing pressure (four signals) and regulatory change (two signals) indicate a supplier under cost and compliance strain, not a ready buyer. This is a low-priority target: the fit gaps and pressure profile outweigh any near-term opening.

findings.sig-0016: Granite Tooling is raising prices on material and labor inflation, signaling cost strain rather than buying capacity for BTX.
findings.sig-0017: New compliance requirements are affecting Granite's certified processes, adding regulatory risk that complicates any engagement.
findings.sig-0018: A second regulatory-change signal reinforces that Granite's certified processes face compliance disruption.
findings.sig-0019: Another price increase confirms sustained cost inflation pressure at Granite, limiting deal readiness.
findings.sig-0020: A further pricing-pressure signal shows the cost-inflation trend is repeated and persistent, raising competitive risk.

apex-machining.opportunity: Apex Machining scores 87, driven by a rapid string of contract wins and government awards totaling over $43M across five deals, plus a facility expansion signaling capacity growth. BTX Precision fits this prospect at 80%, covering 5-axis CNC, AS9100, aluminum, and titanium, with the one gap being NADCAP. The combination of surging demand and BTX's strong capability overlap makes Apex a high-priority target for partnership or overflow work.

findings.sig-0021: Apex is adding machining capacity, indicating growing demand BTX could help absorb as an overflow or subcontract partner.
findings.sig-0022: A $6.0M multi-year production win shows sustained committed volume BTX could support with 5-axis CNC and AS9100 capabilities.
findings.sig-0023: A $10.6M government contract signals defense/gov workload where BTX's titanium and aluminum work fits — but note the NADCAP gap may limit some government requirements.
findings.sig-0024: The $18.7M multi-year contract is the largest booking, representing major long-term production volume and strong overflow potential for BTX.
findings.sig-0025: A $6.1M government award adds to Apex's government backlog; BTX's AS9100 certification aligns, though NADCAP may be needed for some gov scopes.
findings.sig-0026: A $1.7M government award further confirms consistent government demand BTX can position to support.

vanguard-aero.opportunity: Vanguard Aerospace scores 79 on opportunity, driven by three production contract wins ($12.2M, $6.0M, $3.3M) plus a $2.2M government award — a clear surge in booked work that likely exceeds their current capacity. They are a strong fit at 75%, aligning with BTX's ITAR, build-to-print, and titanium capabilities. As a Wichita competitor now expanding capacity (competitivePressure=20), Vanguard is both a subcontracting/overflow prospect and a signal that BTX should defend its position.

findings.sig-0027: Their facility expansion to add machining capacity signals rising demand BTX can help absorb now — and a competitive threat BTX must watch.
findings.sig-0028: The $12.2M multi-year contract creates significant near-term capacity strain — pitch BTX as overflow/build-to-print partner on titanium and ITAR work.
findings.sig-0029: The $6.0M multi-year win adds to their backlog, strengthening the case that they need outside machining support.
findings.sig-0030: The $3.3M multi-year win further stacks demand, increasing the likelihood they subcontract to meet delivery schedules.
findings.sig-0032: The $2.2M government award confirms defense/government work — BTX's ITAR compliance makes it a qualified partner for this scope.

ironclad-comp.opportunity: Ironclad Components is a Hartford-based competitor showing strong momentum, with an opportunity score of 58 driven by two multi-year production contract wins ($12.4M and $14.2M) and a hiring surge signaling active growth. BTX fits this account at 67%, able to serve precision machining and titanium work, though sheet metal is a gap. The combination of new production volume and rapid staffing points to capacity needs BTX can help fill through overflow or subcontract partnerships.

findings.sig-0034: The $12.4M multi-year contract means Ironclad has committed production volume that may exceed internal capacity—an opening for BTX to offer precision machining and titanium support.
findings.sig-0035: A second $14.2M multi-year contract compounds the volume pressure, strengthening the case for BTX as an overflow or subcontract machining partner.
findings.sig-0036: The hiring surge signals Ironclad is scaling to meet demand, indicating capacity gaps BTX can address before their new hires ramp up.

strata-mfg.opportunity: Strata Manufacturing is scaling fast, with two multi-year production contracts ($6.0M and $7.5M) plus a $19.1M government award driving the opportunity score to 68. This contract load is fueling facility expansion to add machining capacity, creating overflow and subcontracting potential where BTX Precision fits 67% (aluminum, build-to-print). As a Phoenix competitor, Strata is both a partnership target for excess demand and a source of competitive pressure (39) worth monitoring.

findings.sig-0037: A $6.0M multi-year production win signals sustained demand that may exceed Strata's capacity, opening build-to-print overflow work for BTX.
findings.sig-0038: Facility expansion for machining capacity shows Strata is investing to compete—monitor as competitive pressure, but also as a sign of workload BTX could support during ramp-up.
findings.sig-0039: A second expansion announcement reinforces that Strata's capacity needs are outpacing current output, increasing subcontracting opportunity for BTX's aluminum capabilities.
findings.sig-0040: A $7.5M multi-year contract adds to Strata's backlog, strengthening the case that they need partners to meet production commitments.
findings.sig-0041: A third expansion signal confirms aggressive capacity growth—engage early to capture spillover before Strata's new capacity comes fully online.
findings.sig-0042: The $19.1M government award is the largest demand driver; government work often requires qualified build-to-print partners, a direct fit for BTX.

harbor-aero.opportunity: Harbor Aerosystems hit a maxed-out opportunity score of 100, driven by two multi-year production contract wins ($4.1M and $19.3M), repeated demand spikes on new program starts, and hiring surges signaling a clear growth push. As an existing customer with 67% fit (BTX can serve their AS9100 and aluminum needs, with a gap only in sheet metal), Harbor is a prime expansion target. Note a moderate capacity risk of 16 tied to their demand spikes, so move quickly before their throughput tightens.

findings.sig-0043: Their hiring surge signals a growth push—engage now to attach BTX capacity to new headcount and programs.

findings.sig-0047: Their $4.1M multi-year contract win means funded, sustained production work BTX can supply parts for.
findings.sig-0048: The $19.3M multi-year contract win is the biggest driver—target this program for BTX AS9100/aluminum content, noting sheet metal is a gap to fill elsewhere.

continental-def.opportunity: Continental Defense is a maxed-out opportunity, scoring 100 on both opportunity and fit — BTX Precision can fully serve all their needs (5-axis CNC, aluminum, build-to-print, precision machining, titanium) with zero capability gaps. The score is driven by a stack of demand-generating events: a $14.0M multi-year contract win (+30), two demand spikes on new program starts (+25 and +15), a $19.3M government contract award (+20), and a hiring surge (+10). With new programs ramping and a capacityRisk of 16 signaling they may be stretched, this is a well-timed target for outsourced precision machining.

findings.sig-0049: Their hiring surge signals a growth push — reach out now to position BTX as overflow machining capacity before they staff up internally.
findings.sig-0050: A demand spike on new program starts means immediate build-to-print and precision machining volume BTX can absorb.

findings.sig-0053: The $14.0M multi-year production contract creates long-run, repeatable machining volume — pitch BTX for a standing supply role.

northwind-sys.opportunity: Northwind Systems is a maxed-out opportunity (score 100) driven by a $17.4M multi-year production contract, a demand spike on new program starts, three government contract awards ($2.7M, $11.1M, $10.9M), and a hiring surge — all pointing to rapid growth and expanding production needs. However, BTX Precision's fit is 0%: we can serve none of their current requirements, with gaps in NADCAP, assembly, and sheet metal. This is a high-intent account to engage now, but only if BTX can close the capability gaps that currently block us from serving them.

findings.sig-0054: Their hiring surge signals a growth push and rising production capacity needs BTX could support if capability gaps are closed.

findings.sig-0058: An $11.1M government award further expands their production load and reinforces qualification requirements BTX doesn't yet meet.
findings.sig-0059: A $10.9M government award compounds the demand, making this a large-scale prospect once BTX addresses NADCAP, assembly, and sheet metal gaps.

beacon-aviation.opportunity: Beacon Aviation is a maximum-priority target with a perfect opportunity score of 100, driven by a $3.7M multi-year production contract win, a $5.8M government contract award, dual demand spikes on new program starts, and a hiring surge. This convergence of new work and growth signals strong, near-term demand for outside machining capacity. BTX fits at 50% — we can serve their titanium needs but have a gap on sheet metal, so lead with titanium.
